### AGENTS.md

# AGENTS.md — Agent (Coding) Mode

This file provides guidance to agents when working with code in this repository.

## Coding Rules (Non-Obvious)

- **Never inline token fetching.** All watsonx IAM → bearer exchanges go through the dedicated `token-manager` module. Route handlers must import it, never call IAM directly.
- **DB partial unique index is the lock.** Do NOT add app-level "check if pending fork exists before inserting." The partial unique index (`WHERE status='proposed'`) is the real guard; duplicate checks create a race window.
- **Character offsets only.** Use `doc.textBetween()` for all anchor calculations — not ProseMirror internal positions. Any helper that computes `anchor_start`/`anchor_end` must use this method.
- **Granite structured output, always.** Prompt must explicitly request `{"alternative": "..."}` JSON only. Parse defensively: missing field or invalid JSON → set `status=failed`, never store partial/garbled content.
- **`/resolved` after every mutation.** After any approve/reject/switch-branch mutation, the caller must re-fetch `GET /document/:id/resolved` and replace the editor content. Never compute the resolved document client-side.
- **Siblings become inactive on approve.** When a fork is approved (`is_active=true`), all other forks sharing the same `(document_id, anchor point)` must be set `is_active=false` in the same transaction.
- **Rejected forks are never deleted.** `status=resolved`, `is_active=false` — stored permanently so tree view can show them.
- **Rate limiter placement.** Token-bucket middleware goes only on the alternative-generation endpoint, not globally.
- **Dev cache:** Granite response cache (keyed on `selected_text + instruction`) is a dev-only flag. It must not run in production — guard it with `NODE_ENV !== 'production'`.

# Project Instructions (AGENTS.md)

# AGENTS.md

This file provides guidance to agents when working with code in this repository.

## Project

**Ledger** — a writing app where every AI-assisted alternative becomes a node in a navigable decision tree. See [`implementation-plan.md`](implementation-plan.md) for the full spec. The project has not been scaffolded yet; no build/test commands exist.

## Stack

- **Frontend:** React + Tiptap (editor); character offsets from `doc.textBetween()` — NOT raw ProseMirror positions
- **Backend:** Node/Express
- **Database:** SQLite with partial unique indexes (the lock/active-fork invariants live at the DB layer, not app layer)
- **AI:** IBM Granite via watsonx.ai (structured output only — `{"alternative": "..."}`, never free-form)
- **Auth:** IAM key → bearer token via a dedicated `token-manager` module; never inline in route handlers

## Critical Architectural Rules

- **Tree resolution is server-side only.** `GET /document/:id/resolved` computes the active document. The frontend never resolves the tree — it re-fetches after every mutation and re-renders.
- **Lock via DB, not app logic.** The partial unique index (`status='proposed'` per document) is the actual concurrency guard — never a "check then insert" pattern.
- **Character offsets** use `doc.textBetween()` (plain-text offsets), not raw ProseMirror positions. This matters everywhere `anchor_start`/`anchor_end` are stored or read.
- **Granite responses must be parsed defensively.** If the response is missing/malformed, the fork transitions to `status=failed`, never stores garbled content.
- **One pending fork at a time.** While `status=proposed` exists on a document, the document is fully locked — no editing, no new forks.

## Data Model Non-Obvious Details

- `Fork.is_active` and `Fork.status` are separate: a rejected fork has `status=resolved`, `is_active=false` and is **kept** in the tree, never deleted.
- Partial unique index: at most one `is_active=true` fork per `(document_id, anchor point)` — siblings at same anchor are mutually exclusive.
- Hard cap on `branch_content`/`why` length (prevent unbounded AI output from corrupting future drift-detection prompts).
- `updated_at` on Fork is for debugging stuck locks only.

## Failure Handling (required, not optional)

- Input cap: reject selections > ~2000 chars before reaching Granite.
- 20s server-side timeout on Granite calls → auto-transition to `status=failed`, auto-unlock.
- Manual cancel action (marks fork failed, unlocks immediately).
- `POST /document/:id/force-unlock` — marks any pending fork as failed, unlocks unconditionally (no auth needed, safety hatch only).

## Dev-Only Patterns

- Granite response cache keyed on `(selected_text + instruction)` during development — do not ship.
- CORS explicit allowlist from the start (frontend dev port ≠ backend port).
- `.env` + `.gitignore` committed before any key is pasted in; watsonx key never touches frontend bundles.
- Rate limiting: in-memory token-bucket middleware, per-session, on the alternative-generation endpoint only.

## Phased Delivery

Build and verify each phase before moving to the next (P1 → P2 → P3 → P4 → P5 stretch). See [`implementation-plan.md`](implementation-plan.md) for phase definitions.

</project_rules>

Contents of file implementation-plan.md:

1 | # Ledger — Implementation Plan
2 | 
3 | ## Overview
4 | 
5 | Ledger is a writing app where a creator writes directly in a live text editor. 
6 | Instead of silently overwriting their own work when trying alternatives, every 
7 | "try something different" moment becomes a tracked, reversible decision in a 
8 | navigable tree.
9 | 
10 | Core interaction: select a passage, request an AI-generated alternative, 
11 | approve or reject it, and later surf between any past decision and the paths 
12 | not taken — never losing anything, never auto-editing anything without approval.
13 | 
14 | ## Core Concept
15 | 
16 | The document is not a static, mutable string. It is the result of walking a 
17 | tree of decisions from an original root, applying whichever branch is "active" 
18 | at each fork point. Rejected alternatives are never deleted — they stay in the 
19 | tree, viewable and re-selectable at any time. Switching which fork is active 
20 | at any point recomputes what the document looks like.
21 | 
22 | ## Data Model
23 | 
24 | ### Document
25 | | Field | Type | Notes |
26 | |---|---|---|
27 | | id | string | primary key |
28 | | title | string | |
29 | | root_content | text | the original, untouched content |
30 | | created_at | timestamp | |
31 | 
32 | ### Fork
33 | | Field | Type | Notes |
34 | |---|---|---|
35 | | id | string | primary key |
36 | | document_id | string | foreign key |
37 | | parent_fork_id | string, nullable | null if attached directly to root; offsets are relative to the parent's resolved output, not to root_content globally |
38 | | anchor_start | integer | plain-text character offset within the **parent's resolved content** — derived from Tiptap's `doc.textBetween()`, NOT raw ProseMirror positions |
39 | | anchor_end | integer | see above |
40 | | original_snippet | text | frozen snapshot of what was there |
41 | | branch_content | text | AI-generated alternative; mutated in-place by direct edits (not replaced with a new fork row) |
42 | | why | text, nullable | AI-drafted summary, editable/confirmable by user; nullable — a fork with why=null is valid and normal |
43 | | status | enum | `proposed` \| `resolved` \| `failed` |
44 | | is_active | boolean | true if this branch is currently live in the document |
45 | | created_at | timestamp | |
46 | | updated_at | timestamp | for debugging stuck locks only — no business logic |
47 | 
48 | ### Database-level constraints
49 | - Partial unique index: at most one `Fork` per `document_id` WHERE `status='proposed'`
50 | - Partial unique index: at most one `Fork` per `(document_id, anchor point)` WHERE `is_active=true`
51 | - Hard cap on `branch_content` / `why` length (prevents unbounded AI output from corrupting resolution or blowing up later drift-detection prompts)
52 | 
53 | ### Single hardcoded document
54 | The app operates on one document, seeded at DB startup with sample text using
55 | `INSERT OR IGNORE`. No `POST /document` endpoint, no document list, no routing.
56 | 
57 | ## Tree Resolution — Server-Side Only
58 | 
59 | The frontend never computes tree logic.
60 | 
61 | **`GET /document/:id/resolved`**
62 | 
63 | Returns a **segment array** — not a flat string. Each segment:
64 | ```
65 | { text: string, fork_id: string | null, start: number, end: number }
66 | ```
67 | where `start`/`end` are offsets within the concatenated resolved document.
68 | The frontend joins segments to produce the display string and uses segment
69 | tags to determine `parent_fork_id` and compute offsets on selection.
70 | 
71 | Resolution algorithm: **recursive descent down the active path.** At each
72 | level, apply the active fork's `branch_content` at `[anchor_start, anchor_end]`
73 | relative to *that level's already-resolved text*, then recurse into children.
74 | Offsets are never relative to `root_content` globally — each level's offsets
75 | are in its own coordinate space. If a parent fork is `is_active=false`, its
76 | entire subtree is skipped. The walk treats `failed` and rejected
77 | (`status=resolved, is_active=false`) forks identically — both are skipped.
78 | 
79 | The frontend calls this after every mutation (approve, reject, switch-branch,
80 | direct edit) and simply re-renders — it does not resolve the tree itself.
81 | 
82 | **`GET /document/:id/tree`**
83 | Returns the flat list of all `Fork` rows for this document (with
84 | `parent_fork_id`, `is_active`, `status`, `original_snippet`, `branch_content`,
85 | `why`) so the frontend can build the visual map. Frontend computes depth and
86 | layout from `parent_fork_id` + `is_active` client-side — no server-side layout
87 | computation.
88 | 
89 | ## Editing Rules
90 | 
91 | Once any fork exists on a document, free-form typing is blocked **except**:
92 | 
93 | 1. **Append at end:** typing new content after the last segment is always
94 |    allowed — no existing `anchor_start`/`anchor_end` is affected. Detected
95 |    client-side by checking cursor position ≥ `end` of the final segment in
96 |    the `/resolved` segment array. Writes to the entity owning the last segment
97 |    (`root_content` if last segment is root, or the owning fork's `branch_content`).
98 | 
99 | 2. **In-segment direct edit:** a "edit directly" action (no Granite call)
100 |    replaces a selection within a single segment. Mutates the owning entity's
101 |    content in-place (`root_content` or `branch_content`). After persisting,
102 |    shifts `anchor_start`/`anchor_end` only for forks whose `parent_fork_id`
103 |    matches the edited segment's `fork_id` (or `null` for root) — never forks
104 |    in sibling branches or unrelated subtrees, as their offsets live in a
105 |    different coordinate space.
106 | 
107 | **Cross-segment selections are rejected hard** — frontend disables/warns if
108 | a selection crosses a segment boundary; backend validates and returns an error.
109 | 
110 | Both operations go through `PATCH /document/:id/edit`:
111 | ```
112 | Body: { segment_fork_id: string | null, anchor_start: number, anchor_end: number, replacement: string }
113 | ```
114 | Append is a zero-length selection (`anchor_start === anchor_end`) at the end
115 | of the last segment. Same document lock rule applies — no pending fork allowed.
116 | 
117 | ## Autosave & Debounce
118 | 
119 | All edits (direct edits, appends) are debounced ~500ms using P1's autosave
120 | mechanism (or a fresh ~500ms debounce if P1's can't be extended cleanly).
121 | 
122 | **Critical invariant:** flush the debounce buffer synchronously and await the
123 | `PATCH` success response before beginning any fork operation (generate
124 | alternative, approve, reject, switch-branch). Fork operations must never begin
125 | against stale persisted content, as offset calculations depend on it.
126 | 
127 | ## Locking Behavior
128 | 
129 | Only one fork can be pending (`status=proposed`) at a time per document.
130 | While a fork is pending, the entire document is locked — no editing anywhere
131 | until it's resolved (approved, rejected, or failed). This avoids offset drift
132 | in `anchor_start`/`anchor_end` and avoids handling overlapping pending forks,
133 | which is out of scope for this build.
134 | 
135 | ## Core Flow
136 | 
137 | 1. Writer types directly into the editor. When no forks exist, this edits
138 |    `root_content` via autosave. When forks exist, only append-at-end and
139 |    in-segment direct edits are permitted (see Editing Rules above).
140 | 2. Writer selects a range of text within a single segment, clicks
141 |    "show alternative." Frontend flushes any pending debounced save and awaits
142 |    success before proceeding.
143 | 3. Frontend immediately disables the "generate alternative" action
144 |    (optimistic UI lock) to prevent double-submission before the backend
145 |    responds.
146 | 4. Frontend sends `{ segment_fork_id, anchor_start, anchor_end, selected_text }`
147 |    to the generate-alternative endpoint. `segment_fork_id` is read from the
148 |    segment tag in the `/resolved` array — the frontend does not re-derive it.
149 |    Backend validates that the selection falls within a single segment.
150 | 5. Backend attempts to `INSERT` a new `Fork` row (`status=proposed`,
151 |    `is_active=false`) as a single atomic operation relying on the partial
152 |    unique index to reject a second concurrent pending fork — never a
153 |    "check then insert" pattern, to avoid a race window. If the insert is
154 |    rejected, return a clean "a fork is already pending" error.
155 | 6. Backend calls IBM Granite (via watsonx) with the selected text, explicitly
156 |    instructing it to return ONLY the replacement text as structured output
157 |    (e.g. `{"alternative": "..."}`) — no preamble, no multiple options, no
158 |    markdown. Backend parses defensively: if the response isn't valid/expected
159 |    structure, or the field is missing, the fork transitions to `status=failed`
160 |    rather than storing garbled content.
161 | 7. Document locks entirely. Frontend shows `original_snippet` vs.
162 |    `branch_content` side-by-side for review.
163 | 8. Writer approves or rejects:
164 |    - **Approve:** In a single transaction: `is_active=true` on this fork,
165 |      `is_active=false` on any sibling forks at the same anchor point,
166 |      `status=resolved`. Document unlocks immediately. Frontend re-fetches
167 |      `/resolved` and re-renders. `why` generation is triggered asynchronously
168 |      as a non-blocking background call (see Why Field below) — it does not
169 |      hold the lock.
170 |    - **Reject:** `status=resolved`, `is_active` stays `false`. Fork remains
171 |      stored and viewable in the tree. Document unlocks, unchanged.
172 | 9. **Tree/map view:** renders all forks as a navigable tree using
173 |    `/document/:id/tree` data. Clicking a node shows its `original_snippet`,
174 |    `branch_content`, and `why` (or "no reason recorded" if `why=null`, with a
175 |    manual "generate why" action). A "switch to this branch" action on any
176 |    non-active fork sets `is_active=true` on it and `is_active=false` on its
177 |    siblings — descendant `is_active` values are preserved as remembered
178 |    decisions on that branch (intentional). Backend recomputes via `/resolved`,
179 |    editor updates to reflect the new active path.
180 | 
181 | ## The `why` Field
182 | 
183 | `why` is nullable. A fork with `why=null` is fully valid — this occurs for
184 | rejected forks (never reached approval), forks reactivated via switch-branch,
185 | and forks where async generation failed.
186 | 
187 | **Lifecycle:**
188 | - Generated asynchronously after approve via `POST /fork/:id/why`
189 | - `status=resolved` + `is_active=true` happen atomically at approval;
190 |   `why` is populated afterward, no new status value needed
191 | - In-flight `why` writes complete and save regardless of subsequent branch
192 |   switches — `why` belongs to the fork row permanently
193 | - Switch-branch reuses whatever `why` is already on the row (no auto-generation)
194 | - Tree view shows `why=null` as "no reason recorded" with a manual
195 |   "generate why" button — never blank or broken-looking
196 | 
197 | **`POST /fork/:id/why`**
198 | - Calls Granite with `original_snippet` + `branch_content`, structured output
199 |   `{"why": "..."}`
200 | - Hard length cap before persisting
201 | - Idempotent — overwrites existing `why` on repeat calls
202 | - Failure returns error + retry affordance; `why` stays `null`, no status
203 |   regression on the fork
204 | - Guarded by same `NODE_ENV !== 'production'` dev cache as other Granite calls
205 | 
206 | ## Reliability & Failure Handling
207 | 
208 | Build this alongside P2–P3, not as an afterthought.
209 | 
210 | - **Input caps:** reject any selection/generation request over ~2000
211 |   characters before it reaches Granite. Return a clear validation error.
212 |   Enforced server-side (not just client-side).
213 | - **Server-side timeout** on the Granite call (~20s). On timeout, the fork
214 |   auto-transitions to `status=failed` and the document auto-unlocks — a
215 |   pending fork must never lock the document indefinitely.
216 | - **Manual cancel action:** while a fork is proposed and awaiting the Granite
217 |   response or user review, expose a "cancel" action that marks it failed and
218 |   unlocks the document immediately, without waiting out the timeout.
219 | - **Manual force-unlock:** `POST /document/:id/force-unlock` — a single
220 |   `UPDATE forks SET status='failed' WHERE document_id=? AND status='proposed'`.
221 |   No cascade, no parent state changes. Resolution walk already skips non-active
222 |   forks regardless of status. Safety hatch for live demo situations — no
223 |   auth/polish needed, it just needs to work.
224 | - **Race protection** relies on the DB partial unique index, not
225 |   application-level checks, as the actual guarantee against concurrent
226 |   duplicate forks.
227 | - **Dev-only response cache:** cache Granite responses to avoid burning API
228 |   quota on repeated identical test requests. Covers both Granite call types.
229 |   Keys are hashed (collision-safe): alternative generation →
230 |   `hash(original_snippet + instruction_template_id)`, why generation →
231 |   `hash(original_snippet + branch_content + instruction_template_id)`.
232 |   Guarded by `NODE_ENV !== 'production'`. Does not ship in the final build.
233 | 
234 | ## Stack
235 | 
236 | - **Frontend:** React + Tiptap (live text editor with real selection handling)
237 | - **Backend:** Node/Express
238 | - **Database:** SQLite (with partial unique indexes for lock/active-fork invariants)
239 | - **AI:** IBM Granite via watsonx.ai
240 |   - Auth via a dedicated token-manager module (IAM key → bearer token
241 |     exchange, cached, auto-refreshed before ~1hr expiry — never inline in
242 |     route handlers)
243 | - **Rate limiting:** in-memory token-bucket per session (server-issued httpOnly
244 |   session cookie as identity; `Map<sessionId, tokenBucket>`, wiped on restart),
245 |   on the alternative-generation endpoint only
246 | - **Env/secrets:** `.env` + `.gitignore` committed in the first scaffold
247 |   commit, before any key is pasted in. Watsonx key never touches frontend
248 |   code/bundles.
249 | - **CORS:** `CORS_ORIGIN` in `.env`, read at Express startup
250 |   (e.g. `CORS_ORIGIN=http://localhost:5173` for dev). For demo deployment,
251 |   React build is served statically from Express (same origin); `CORS_ORIGIN`
252 |   unset → CORS middleware disabled entirely. No hardcoded ports in source.
253 | 
254 | ## API Surface
255 | 
256 | | Method | Path | Purpose |
257 | |---|---|---|
258 | | GET | `/document/:id/resolved` | Segment array of resolved document |
259 | | GET | `/document/:id/tree` | Flat list of all fork rows |
260 | | PATCH | `/document/:id/edit` | Direct edit or append within a segment |
261 | | POST | `/document/:id/generate-alternative` | Insert proposed fork, call Granite |
262 | | POST | `/fork/:id/approve` | Approve fork (atomic), unlock document |
263 | | POST | `/fork/:id/reject` | Reject fork, unlock document |
264 | | POST | `/fork/:id/cancel` | Cancel proposed fork, unlock document |
265 | | POST | `/fork/:id/why` | Generate (or regenerate) why field async |
266 | | POST | `/fork/:id/switch` | Switch active branch at this fork's level |
267 | | POST | `/document/:id/force-unlock` | Mark pending fork failed, unconditional unlock |
268 | 
269 | ## Scope for This Build
270 | 
271 | - No auto-detection of decision points — user manually selects text and
272 |   requests an alternative.
273 | - New forks can only be created on the currently active path (selecting within
274 |   a single active segment).
275 | - No multi-user collaboration, no auth beyond the force-unlock safety hatch —
276 |   single-session, single-document demo is fine.
277 | - No persistence requirements beyond SQLite surviving a dev session.
278 | - Drift detection is a stretch goal only — not built until P1–P4 are fully
279 |   working.
280 | 
281 | ## Phased Delivery
282 | 
283 | | Phase | Deliverable | Status |
284 | |---|---|---|
285 | | P1 | Single hardcoded document seeded with sample text. Live editor with ~500ms autosave debounce, editing `root_content` when no forks exist. `GET /document/:id/resolved` returns segment array. | ⬜ |
286 | | P2 | Segment-aware selection (cross-segment rejected). Flush debounce before fork ops. `POST /generate-alternative` with structured-output extraction, input cap, 20s timeout, `status=failed` on bad parse. Lock document, show original vs. proposed side-by-side. Session cookie issued for rate limiter. | ⬜ |
287 | | P3 | Approve (atomic `is_active`/sibling swap/`status=resolved`, immediate unlock, async `why` via `POST /fork/:id/why`). Reject. Cancel. Force-unlock. `PATCH /document/:id/edit` with coordinate-space-scoped offset shifting. Append-at-end detection. | ⬜ |
288 | | P4 | Tree/map view from `GET /document/:id/tree`. Clickable nodes showing `original_snippet`, `branch_content`, `why` (with "no reason recorded" + manual generate-why for `why=null`). Working switch-branch (sibling swap only, descendant state preserved). | ⬜ |
289 | | P5 (stretch) | Drift detection — compare new content against past resolved forks' `why` reasoning, flag a plain-language contradiction without auto-editing | ⬜ |
290 | 
291 | Build and verify each phase before moving to the next.
292 |

# Ledger

A writing app where every AI-assisted alternative becomes a node in a navigable decision tree.

## Quick start

### 1. Environment

Copy `.env` and fill in your watsonx credentials when needed for P2+:

```sh
# .env is already present — edit if needed
PORT=3001
CORS_ORIGIN=http://localhost:5173
```

### 2. Backend

```sh
cd server
npm install
npm run dev        # node --watch, auto-restarts on file changes
```

Server starts at `http://localhost:3001`. SQLite DB is created at `server/ledger.db` on first run and seeded with a hardcoded document (`doc_hardcoded_001`).

### 3. Frontend

```sh
cd client
npm install
npm run dev        # Vite dev server at http://localhost:5173
```

Open `http://localhost:5173`. The editor loads the seeded document and autosaves every ~500 ms.

## API (P1)

| Method | Path | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| GET | `/document/:id` | Raw document row |
| GET | `/document/:id/resolved` | Segment array of resolved document |
| PATCH | `/document/:id/content` | Autosave `root_content` (P1 only) |

### Segment array shape

```json
{
  "segments": [
    { "text": "...", "fork_id": null, "start": 0, "end": 566 }
  ]
}
```

For P1 (no forks), always one segment with `fork_id: null`.

## Project structure

```
server/
  src/
    db.js        — SQLite init, schema, seed
    resolve.js   — segment-array resolution (recursive descent)
    index.js     — Express app + routes
client/
  src/
    main.jsx     — React entry
    App.jsx      — loads resolved document on mount
    Editor.jsx   — Tiptap editor + 500ms autosave debounce
    Editor.css   — editor styles
implementation-plan.md  — full spec + phase plan
```

## Phases

| Phase | Status |
|---|---|
| P1 — Schema, seed, live editor, `/resolved` segment array | ✅ |
| P2 — Alternative generation, Granite, locking | ⬜ |
| P3 — Approve, reject, cancel, force-unlock, `PATCH /edit` | ⬜ |
| P4 — Tree/map view, switch-branch | ⬜ |
| P5 — Drift detection (stretch) | ⬜ |

data.content:
P1 is complete. Here's a summary of everything that was built:

---

## What was implemented

### Repo structure
```
server/src/db.js        — SQLite schema, seed, singleton
server/src/resolve.js   — segment-array resolution (recursive descent)
server/src/index.js     — Express app + routes
client/src/main.jsx     — React entry
client/src/App.jsx      — fetches /resolved on mount
client/src/Editor.jsx   — Tiptap editor + autosave
client/src/Editor.css   — clean minimal styles
```

### Backend (Node/Express + `node:sqlite`)

- **`node:sqlite`** — Node 25's built-in SQLite module; zero native addons, no compilation.
- **Schema** ([`server/src/db.js`](server/src/db.js)):
  - `documents` table with `root_content`
  - `forks` table with `CHECK` constraints (status enum, is_active 0/1, `branch_content ≤ 8000 chars`, `why ≤ 2000 chars`)
  - `uq_one_proposed_per_doc` — partial unique index `WHERE status='proposed'` (the concurrency lock)
  - `uq_one_active_per_anchor` — partial unique index `WHERE is_active=1` (sibling exclusion)
- **Seed** — `INSERT OR IGNORE` at startup; idempotent across restarts
- **`GET /document/:id/resolved`** ([`server/src/resolve.js`](server/src/resolve.js)) — recursive descent algorithm already written for the full P3 case; returns `[{ text, fork_id: null, start: 0, end: N }]` for P1
- **`PATCH /document/:id/content`** — autosave endpoint for root_content

### Frontend (React + Tiptap + Vite)

- [`Editor.jsx`](client/src/Editor.jsx) — Tiptap editor using **`doc.textBetween()`** for character offset extraction (per spec), with a clean 500ms debounced `persistContent` callback
- Save status indicator: `Saving…` / `Saved` / `Save failed`
- Vite dev server proxies `/document` → `localhost:3001`, so no CORS issues during development

### Verified
```
GET  /resolved   → 1 segment, fork_id: null, start: 0, end: 566 ✓
PATCH /content   → {"ok":true} ✓
Round-trip       → patched content reflected in next /resolved ✓
Vite build       → 118 modules, no errors ✓
```
